docs.title: crdb-dump
docs.text:
Export and import CockroachDB schemas and data — SQL, JSON, YAML, or chunked CSV.

A feature-rich CLI for dumping and restoring CockroachDB: parallel chunked exports, manifest checksums, multi-schema (non-public) objects, rich type support (BYTES/UUID/ARRAY/VECTOR), permissions, resumable imports, S3-compatible storage, region-aware filtering, and automatic retries.

What crdb-dump is — and isn't

crdb-dump is a logical, point-in-time dump/restore tool. It is not a live-migration tool. For minimal-downtime or continuous migration, use CockroachDB MOLT or Logical Data Replication (LDR). See Migration & Limitations.

docs.title: Schema export
docs.text: Tables, views, sequences, and enum types in any schema (not just public). Full-database dumps use native SHOW CREATE ALL TYPES / SHOW CREATE ALL TABLES — dependency-ordered, with foreign keys validated after load.

docs.title: Data export
docs.text: CSV or SQL, with chunking, gzip, ordering, parallelism, and per-chunk SHA-256 manifests.

docs.title: Rich types
docs.text: BYTES, UUID, STRING[]/arrays, TIMESTAMP, enums, and VECTOR all round-trip in both SQL and CSV.

docs.title: Resumable imports
docs.text: COPY-based loads with per-chunk resume logs, parallel loading, CSV validation, and dry runs — restarts are safe and idempotent.

docs.title: S3-compatible storage
docs.text: Stream chunks to and from AWS S3, MinIO, or Cohesity with custom endpoints.

docs.title: Region-aware
docs.text: Filter export and import by table locality in multi-region databases.

docs.title: Quick example
docs.text:
export CRDB_URL="cockroachdb://root@localhost:26257/mydb?sslmode=disable"

# Export full schema + chunked CSV data
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --data-format=csv --chunk-size=1000

# Restore into a fresh database
crdb-dump load --db=mydb \
  --schema=crdb_dump_output/mydb/mydb_schema.sql \
  --data-dir=crdb_dump_output/mydb --validate-csv

docs.title: Get started
docs.text:
• Getting Started

    Install crdb-dump and run your first export and restore.

    Quick Start

  • Guides

    Learn every feature: schema, data, types, S3, regions, and more.

    Guides

  • Recipes

    Task-oriented, copy-paste workflows for common jobs.

    Recipes

  • CLI Reference

    Every command and option, generated from the CLI.

    CLI Reference

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
pip install crdb-dump

Requires Python 3.10+.

docs.title: Community & support
docs.text:
• Issues and feature requests: https://github.com/viragtripathi/crdb-dump/issues
  • Contributing
  • Released under the MIT License

docs.title: Unreleased
docs.text: No changes yet.

docs.title: Added
docs.text: • --as-of-system-time on export: read all table data at one pinned cluster timestamp for a consistent point-in-time snapshot. The bare flag pins cluster_logical_timestamp(); an explicit value (interval, timestamp, or decimal) is used verbatim. The pinned timestamp is recorded in each manifest as as_of_system_time.

docs.title: Breaking
docs.text:
• Object naming is now three-part database.schema.table everywhere (filenames, manifests, resume-log keys, and --tables input). Pre-0.4.0 two-part dumps are not compatible with the 0.4.0 loader.
  • --tables two-part input now means schema.table (database taken from --db), not the legacy database.table.
  • Minimum supported Python is now 3.10.

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• Export and restore now work for objects in non-public schemas (the data exporter previously crashed on three-part names, and schema export silently dropped the schema component).
  • Mixed-case and reserved-word identifiers are now correctly quoted.
  • load honors --host/--port/--certs-dir (the psycopg2 path previously ignored them and only read CRDB_URL/localhost).
  • Schema export no longer duplicates DDL when the aggregate file already exists.
  • Schema load splitting handles semicolons inside string literals and function bodies (via sqlparse).
  • Selective data export (--tables) resolves the database from --db instead of misreading the first name segment as the database.
  • Replaced deprecated datetime.utcnow().

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Native SHOW CREATE ALL TYPES / SHOW CREATE ALL TABLES for full-database dumps (dependency-ordered, with foreign-key constraints validated post-load).
  • Centralized identifier model (crdb_dump/utils/identifiers.py) used across all modules for naming and safe SQL quoting.
  • Regression tests for CockroachDB VECTOR data round-trips (SQL and CSV).

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Dependencies upgraded to current GA releases (SQLAlchemy 2.0.51, sqlalchemy-cockroachdb 2.0.4, click 8.4.2, PyYAML 6.0.3, psycopg2-binary 2.9.12, boto3 1.43.36, sqlparse 0.5.5).
  • Data chunk files renamed from <table>_chunk_NNN.* to <db.schema.table>_NNN.*; manifests to <db.schema.table>.manifest.json.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Pull requests are welcome — star ⭐ the repo, file issues, or propose features at https://github.com/viragtripathi/crdb-dump/issues.

docs.title: Development setup
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/viragtripathi/crdb-dump
cd crdb-dump
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: Before opening a PR
docs.text:
• Run unit tests: pytest -m "not integration"
  • If your change touches export/import behavior, run the integration and end-to-end suites too — see Testing.
  • Keep commit messages plain and descriptive.

For releases, see Releasing.

docs.title: Releasing
docs.text: Releases publish to PyPI via the Release GitHub Action (.github/workflows/release.yml) using PyPI Trusted Publishing (OIDC) — no API tokens are stored in the repo.

docs.title: One-time setup
docs.text:
On PyPI, add a Trusted Publisher for the crdb-dump project:

  • Owner: viragtripathi
  • Repository: crdb-dump
  • Workflow: release.yml

docs.title: Cutting a release
docs.text:
1. Bump version in pyproject.toml.
  2. In CHANGELOG.md, rename the ## Unreleased section to ## <version> — <YYYY-MM-DD> and add a fresh empty ## Unreleased section above it.
  3. Merge to main.
  4. Run Actions → Release → Run workflow and enter the same version (e.g. 0.4.0).

The workflow verifies the input matches the packaged version, runs the full test suite against a CockroachDB container, builds the sdist/wheel, publishes to PyPI, and creates a v<version> GitHub Release with auto-generated notes.

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
crdb-dump is tested at three levels. Requires Python 3.10+.

python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: Unit tests (no database)
docs.text: pytest -m "not integration"

docs.title: Integration tests (need a reachable CockroachDB)
docs.text:
export CRDB_URL="cockroachdb://root@localhost:26257/defaultdb?sslmode=disable"
pytest -m integration

Integration tests cover non-public schema, VECTOR, and BYTES round-trips.

docs.title: End-to-end
docs.text:
./test-local.sh

The script auto-detects a single- or multi-region cluster, exercises export → verify → restore → resume, and an S3 (MinIO) round-trip. It resolves the CLI from the repo .venv automatically.

docs.title: Connection
docs.text:
crdb-dump connects via the CRDB_URL environment variable (preferred), otherwise it defaults to localhost:26257:

export CRDB_URL="cockroachdb://root@localhost:26257/mydb?sslmode=disable"
# postgresql:// URLs are also accepted and normalized automatically
export CRDB_URL="postgresql://root@host:26257/mydb?sslmode=disable"

docs.title: TLS / secure clusters
docs.text:
When CRDB_URL is not set, you can point at a certificates directory:

crdb-dump export --db=mydb --certs-dir ~/certs

This connects with sslmode=verify-full using ca.crt, client.root.crt, and client.root.key from that directory.

Use --print-connection to print the resolved connection URL (credentials redacted) and exit.

docs.title: S3 / object storage
docs.text: S3 credentials can be supplied via flags or the standard AWS environment variables AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID / AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY. See S3-Compatible Storage for the full set of options (custom endpoints for MinIO and Cohesity, bucket, prefix).

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pip install crdb-dump

Requires Python 3.10+.

docs.title: From source (development)
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/viragtripathi/crdb-dump
cd crdb-dump
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e ".[dev]"

Verify the install:

crdb-dump version

docs.title: 1. Point at a cluster
docs.text: export CRDB_URL="cockroachdb://root@localhost:26257/mydb?sslmode=disable"

docs.title: 2. Export schema + data
docs.text:
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --data-format=csv --chunk-size=1000

Output lands under crdb_dump_output/mydb/:

  • mydb_schema.sql — full DDL (dependency-ordered, with CREATE SCHEMA for non-public schemas)
  • mydb.<schema>.<table>_NNN.csv — chunked data
  • mydb.<schema>.<table>.manifest.json — checksummed chunk manifest

docs.title: 3. Restore
docs.text:
crdb-dump load --db=mydb \
  --schema=crdb_dump_output/mydb/mydb_schema.sql \
  --data-dir=crdb_dump_output/mydb \
  --validate-csv

docs.title: 4. Verify checksums
docs.text:
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --verify

Next: read the Guides for the full feature set, or the CLI Reference for every option.

docs.title: Guides
docs.text:
In-depth guides for every crdb-dump feature.

  • Export Schema

    Full-database DDL, formats, per-object files, and object selection.

    Export Schema

  • Export Data

    CSV/SQL, chunking, gzip, ordering, parallelism, and manifests.

    Export Data

  • Import & Restore

    Schema apply, COPY loads, validation, parallelism, and resume.

    Import & Restore

  • Multi-Schema Objects

    Three-part naming and non-public schema support.

    Multi-Schema Objects

  • Type Handling

    BYTES, UUID, arrays, enums, and VECTOR round-trips.

    Type Handling

  • Permissions

    Export roles, grants, and role memberships.

    Permissions

  • Region-Aware

    Filter export and import by table locality.

    Region-Aware

  • S3-Compatible Storage

    AWS S3, MinIO, and Cohesity with custom endpoints.

    S3-Compatible Storage

  • Migration & Limitations

    What crdb-dump is, what it isn't, and the consistency caveat.

    Migration & Limitations

docs.title: Exporting Data
docs.text: Add --data to export table data alongside (or instead of) schema.

docs.title: Formats
docs.text:
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --data-format=csv    # chunked CSV (default)
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --data-format=sql    # INSERT statements

docs.title: Chunking
docs.text:
Rows are written in chunks; each chunk is a separate file plus an entry in the table's manifest.

crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --data-format=csv --chunk-size=1000

Files are named mydb.<schema>.<table>_NNN.csv. Each table also gets a mydb.<schema>.<table>.manifest.json recording every chunk's row count and SHA-256 checksum.

docs.title: Compression
docs.text: crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --data-format=csv --data-compress   # .csv.gz

docs.title: Ordering
docs.text:
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --data-order=id
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --data-order=id --data-order-desc
# Fail (instead of warn) if an ordered column is missing:
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --data-order=id --data-order-strict

docs.title: Parallelism and limits
docs.text:
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --data-parallel      # export tables concurrently
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --data-limit=100000  # cap rows per table

docs.title: Consistent snapshots (--as-of-system-time)
docs.text:
By default each table is read independently, so a dump of a live database is not consistent across tables. Use --as-of-system-time to read every table (and every chunk) at one pinned cluster timestamp:

# pin one cluster_logical_timestamp() for the whole export
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --as-of-system-time

# or pass an explicit interval / timestamp / decimal
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --as-of-system-time='-30s'

The pinned timestamp is recorded in each manifest as as_of_system_time.

Warning

The timestamp must be within the table's garbage-collection window (gc.ttlseconds, default 25h). A very long export against an old timestamp can fail once the snapshot ages out of GC.

docs.title: Verifying
docs.text:
Re-run with --verify to validate each chunk against its manifest checksum:

crdb-dump export --db=mydb --verify

docs.title: Exporting Schema
docs.text: By default, crdb-dump export writes the full database DDL to crdb_dump_output/<db>/<db>_schema.sql.

docs.title: Full-database DDL
docs.text:
For a whole-database export, crdb-dump uses CockroachDB's native bulk DDL:

crdb-dump export --db=mydb

It emits, in dependency order:

  1. CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS for every non-public schema,
  2. SHOW CREATE ALL TYPES (enums),
  3. SHOW CREATE ALL TABLES (sequences → tables → views, with foreign-key constraints split into trailing ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT ... VALIDATE).

This makes the resulting mydb_schema.sql safe to replay into a fresh database.

docs.title: Output formats
docs.text:
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --format sql     # default
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --format json
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --format yaml

docs.title: One file per object
docs.text:
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --per-table
# e.g. table_mydb.public.users.sql, type_mydb.public.status.sql

docs.title: Selecting objects
docs.text:
# Include specific objects (table / schema.table / db.schema.table)
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --tables=public.users,cpkit.tasks

# Exclude specific objects
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --exclude-tables=public.audit_log

See the Naming Model for how names are interpreted.

docs.title: Permissions
docs.text: Add roles, grants, and role memberships with --include-permissions — see Permissions.

docs.title: Importing & Restoring
docs.text: crdb-dump load applies schema and loads data via CockroachDB COPY.

docs.title: Schema + data
docs.text:
crdb-dump load --db=mydb \
  --schema=crdb_dump_output/mydb/mydb_schema.sql \
  --data-dir=crdb_dump_output/mydb \
  --validate-csv
  • --schema applies a .sql DDL file (statements are split safely, respecting string literals and function bodies).
  • --data-dir loads every *.manifest.json found in the directory.
  • --validate-csv checks each chunk's header against the live table columns before loading.

docs.title: Parallel loading
docs.text: crdb-dump load --db=mydb --data-dir=crdb_dump_output/mydb --parallel-load

docs.title: Dry run
docs.text: crdb-dump load --db=mydb --data-dir=crdb_dump_output/mydb --dry-run

docs.title: Resumable loads
docs.text:
Loads record progress so re-runs skip already-loaded chunks (idempotent):

# single shared log file
crdb-dump load --db=mydb --data-dir=... --resume-log=resume.json

# one log per table
crdb-dump load --db=mydb --data-dir=... --resume-log-dir=resume/

# abort on the first failed chunk
crdb-dump load --db=mydb --data-dir=... --resume-log-dir=resume/ --resume-strict

docs.title: Selecting tables on load
docs.text:
crdb-dump load --db=mydb --data-dir=... --include-tables=mydb.public.users
crdb-dump load --db=mydb --data-dir=... --exclude-tables=mydb.public.audit_log

docs.title: What crdb-dump is
docs.text: A logical, point-in-time dump/restore tool: it exports DDL (SHOW CREATE) and table data (chunked SELECT), and restores via CREATE + COPY. It is ideal for clones, dev/test seeding, selective extracts, and backups of static or quiesced data.

docs.title: What crdb-dump is not
docs.text:
It is not a live-migration tool. It has no change-data-capture, no continuous replication, and no cutover orchestration. For minimal-downtime or continuous migration, use CockroachDB's purpose-built tooling:

  • MOLT (Migrate Off Legacy Technology) — Fetch (bulk load), Replicator (continuous replication), and Verify. The right choice for minimal-downtime migrations.
  • Logical Data Replication (LDR) — continuous, table-level CockroachDB↔CockroachDB replication (self-hosted clusters).

docs.title: CockroachDB Cloud tier moves (Basic → Standard → Advanced)
docs.text: The documented tier-to-tier path is an export/import via cloud storage workflow. crdb-dump can automate that pattern — with a write-freeze window on the source (stop writes → dump → restore). For a live/near-zero-downtime tier move, use MOLT, and for CockroachDB-to-CockroachDB migrations contact your Cockroach Labs account team.

docs.title: Consistency caveat
docs.text:
By default crdb-dump reads each table independently with OFFSET/LIMIT, so a dump taken while the database is being written is not a transactionally consistent snapshot across tables.

Use --as-of-system-time for a consistent snapshot

Pass --as-of-system-time to read every table at one pinned cluster timestamp:

crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --as-of-system-time

The timestamp must stay within the garbage-collection window (gc.ttlseconds), so keep exports shorter than that window or raise the TTL.

docs.title: Further reading
docs.text:
• CockroachDB MOLT
  • Logical Data Replication
  • Migrate from Standard or Basic to Advanced

docs.title: Multi-Schema (non-public) Objects
docs.text: CockroachDB objects are three-part: database.schema.table. crdb-dump is schema-aware everywhere — objects in non-public schemas are exported and restored correctly (filenames, manifests, DDL, and COPY targets are all fully qualified).

docs.title: --tables input forms
docs.text:
Input Interpreted as users <db>.public.users cpkit.tasks <db>.cpkit.tasks mydb.cpkit.tasks mydb.cpkit.tasks

A two-part name is schema.table (the database comes from --db), not the legacy database.table.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
# Export a table in the cpkit schema
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --tables=cpkit.tasks --data --data-format=csv

This produces mydb.cpkit.tasks_001.csv and mydb.cpkit.tasks.manifest.json. A full-database export additionally emits CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS "cpkit"; in the schema file so a fresh-database restore works.

See the Naming Model reference for details.

docs.title: Permissions
docs.text:
Add --include-permissions to export roles, grants, and role memberships alongside the schema:

crdb-dump export --db=mydb --include-permissions

This writes, under crdb_dump_output/mydb/:

  • roles.sql — CREATE ROLE statements
  • grants.sql — object GRANT statements
  • role_memberships.sql — role membership grants (with WITH ADMIN OPTION where applicable)
  • permissions.sql — an aggregate of all of the above, with an export timestamp

Apply them during restore with crdb-dump load --schema=... (or run the SQL directly), after the schema objects exist.

docs.title: Region-Aware Export & Import
docs.text: For multi-region databases, --region filters by table locality on export and by manifest region on import.

docs.title: Export a single region
docs.text:
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --data-format=csv --region=us-east1

Only tables whose locality matches us-east1 are exported. Each manifest records the table's region.

docs.title: Import a single region
docs.text:
crdb-dump load --db=mydb --data-dir=crdb_dump_output/mydb --region=us-east1

Chunks whose manifest region does not match are skipped.

Note

Region metadata comes from SHOW TABLES locality. On a single-region (or non-multi-region) cluster there are no regions to filter on, and --region selects nothing.

docs.title: S3-Compatible Storage
docs.text: crdb-dump can write data chunks to and read them from any S3-compatible store (AWS S3, MinIO, Cohesity) with --use-s3.

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description --use-s3 Enable S3 upload (export) / download (load) of data chunks --s3-bucket Bucket name --s3-prefix Key prefix under which chunks are stored --s3-endpoint Custom endpoint (e.g. MinIO/Cohesity) --s3-access-key Access key (or AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID) --s3-secret-key Secret key (or AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY)

docs.title: Export to MinIO
docs.text:
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --data-format=csv --chunk-size=1000 \
  --use-s3 \
  --s3-bucket=crdb-test-bucket \
  --s3-endpoint=http://localhost:9000 \
  --s3-access-key=minioadmin \
  --s3-secret-key=minioadmin \
  --s3-prefix=backup1/

docs.title: Load from MinIO
docs.text:
crdb-dump load --db=mydb \
  --data-dir=crdb_dump_output/mydb \
  --use-s3 \
  --s3-bucket=crdb-test-bucket \
  --s3-endpoint=http://localhost:9000 \
  --s3-access-key=minioadmin \
  --s3-secret-key=minioadmin \
  --s3-prefix=backup1/ \
  --validate-csv --parallel-load --resume-log-dir=resume/

The schema file is written locally; only data chunks go to S3.

docs.title: Type Handling
docs.text:
crdb-dump preserves CockroachDB data types across export and restore in both SQL and CSV formats.

Type SQL export CSV export BYTES decode('0102', 'hex') bytea hex \x0102 UUID string literal string STRING[] / arrays '{a,b}' array literal {a,b} VECTOR(n) '[1.5,2,3]' string literal [1.5,2,3] (quoted) enums (CREATE TYPE) via SHOW CREATE ALL TYPES value as string

docs.title: BYTES
docs.text: BYTES round-trip safely: SQL exports use decode(..., 'hex'), and CSV exports use the PostgreSQL bytea hex format (\x...) so COPY ... WITH CSV decodes them back to bytes rather than storing the literal characters.

docs.title: VECTOR
docs.text: CockroachDB returns VECTOR values as strings like [1.5,2,3.25]. crdb-dump preserves them verbatim — in CSV the comma-bearing value is quoted, and COPY restores it unchanged. The VECTOR(n) column type itself is emitted by SHOW CREATE.

docs.title: Enums
docs.text: User-defined enum types are exported via SHOW CREATE ALL TYPES and recreated before the tables that use them.

docs.title: Recipes
docs.text:
Task-oriented, copy-paste workflows for common jobs.

  • Full Dump & Restore

    Export a whole database and restore it into a fresh one.

    Full Dump & Restore

  • Selective Tables

    Export and restore a subset of tables or schemas.

    Selective Tables

  • Cross-Environment Copy

    Copy a database from one cluster to another.

    Cross-Environment Copy

  • S3 Round-Trip

    Export to MinIO/S3 and restore from it.

    S3 Round-Trip

  • Verify & Resume

    Validate checksums and resume interrupted loads.

    Verify & Resume

docs.title: Cross-Environment Copy
docs.text:
Copy a database from one cluster to another (e.g. staging → local).

Consistency

crdb-dump takes a logical snapshot without AS OF SYSTEM TIME. Quiesce writes on the source for a consistent copy. See Migration & Limitations.

docs.title: Export from the source
docs.text:
export CRDB_URL="cockroachdb://user@source-host:26257/mydb?sslmode=verify-full&..."
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --data-format=csv --chunk-size=1000 \
  --out-dir=copy_out

docs.title: Load into the target
docs.text:
export CRDB_URL="cockroachdb://root@localhost:26257/mydb?sslmode=disable"
cockroach sql --insecure -e "CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS mydb;"

crdb-dump load --db=mydb \
  --schema=copy_out/mydb/mydb_schema.sql \
  --data-dir=copy_out/mydb \
  --validate-csv --parallel-load --resume-log-dir=resume/

docs.title: Full Database Dump & Restore
docs.text: Export an entire database (schema + data), then restore it into a fresh database.

docs.title: Export
docs.text:
export CRDB_URL="cockroachdb://root@localhost:26257/mydb?sslmode=disable"
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --data-format=csv --chunk-size=1000

Produces crdb_dump_output/mydb/mydb_schema.sql plus per-table chunk files and manifests.

docs.title: Restore into a fresh database
docs.text:
cockroach sql --insecure -e "DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS mydb CASCADE; CREATE DATABASE mydb;"

crdb-dump load --db=mydb \
  --schema=crdb_dump_output/mydb/mydb_schema.sql \
  --data-dir=crdb_dump_output/mydb \
  --validate-csv --parallel-load --resume-log-dir=resume/

The schema file recreates non-public schemas, enum types, sequences, tables, and validates foreign keys.

docs.title: Verify
docs.text:
cockroach sql --insecure -d mydb -e "SELECT count(*) FROM users;"
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --verify

docs.title: S3 Round-Trip (MinIO)
docs.text: Export data chunks to an S3-compatible store and restore from it.

docs.title: Export to MinIO
docs.text:
crdb-dump export --db=mydb --data --data-format=csv --chunk-size=1000 \
  --use-s3 \
  --s3-bucket=crdb-test-bucket \
  --s3-endpoint=http://localhost:9000 \
  --s3-access-key=minioadmin \
  --s3-secret-key=minioadmin \
  --s3-prefix=backup1/ \
  --out-dir=s3_out

docs.title: Restore from MinIO
docs.text:
cockroach sql --insecure -e "DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS mydb CASCADE; CREATE DATABASE mydb;"

# schema is local; data chunks are pulled from S3
crdb-dump load --db=mydb \
  --schema=s3_out/mydb/mydb_schema.sql \
  --data-dir=s3_out/mydb \
  --use-s3 \
  --s3-bucket=crdb-test-bucket \
  --s3-endpoint=http://localhost:9000 \
  --s3-access-key=minioadmin \
  --s3-secret-key=minioadmin \
  --s3-prefix=backup1/ \
  --validate-csv --parallel-load --resume-log-dir=resume/

docs.title: Selective Tables / Schemas
docs.text: Export and restore a subset of objects.

docs.title: Export specific objects
docs.text:
# table / schema.table / db.schema.table forms all work
crdb-dump export --db=mydb \
  --tables=public.users,cpkit.tasks \
  --data --data-format=csv

For schema DDL of the selected objects, add --per-table to get one file each.

docs.title: Restore only some tables
docs.text:
--data-dir loads every manifest it finds; restrict with --include-tables (or exclude with --exclude-tables), using fully-qualified names:

crdb-dump load --db=mydb \
  --data-dir=crdb_dump_output/mydb \
  --include-tables=mydb.cpkit.tasks \
  --validate-csv --resume-log-dir=resume/

docs.title: Verify chunk checksums
docs.text:
After an export, validate every chunk against its manifest SHA-256:

crdb-dump export --db=mydb --verify

docs.title: Resume an interrupted load
docs.text:
Loads record progress per chunk. If a load is interrupted, re-running with the same resume log skips chunks that already succeeded:

# first attempt (interrupted partway)
crdb-dump load --db=mydb --data-dir=crdb_dump_output/mydb \
  --resume-log-dir=resume/ --validate-csv --parallel-load

# re-run — already-loaded chunks are skipped
crdb-dump load --db=mydb --data-dir=crdb_dump_output/mydb \
  --resume-log-dir=resume/ --validate-csv --parallel-load --resume-strict

The second run reports Skipped for chunks already present, making restarts safe and idempotent.

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text:
Reference material for the crdb-dump command line and on-disk formats.

  • Commands

    Every command and option, generated from the CLI.

    Commands

  • Naming Model

    Three-part database.schema.table naming and quoting.

    Naming Model

  • Manifest Format

    The per-table chunk manifest and resume-log keys.

    Manifest Format

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text: The following is generated from the crdb-dump command-line interface.

docs.title: crdb-dump
docs.text:
crdb-dump: Export and Import CockroachDB schemas and data.

Usage:

crdb-dump [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...

Options:

  --verbose  Enable debug logging
  --version  Show the version and exit.
  --help     Show this message and exit.

docs.title: crdb-dump export
docs.text:
Usage:

crdb-dump export [OPTIONS]

Options:

  --db TEXT                 Database name  [required]
  --tables TEXT             Comma-separated list of db.table names
  --exclude-tables TEXT     Comma-separated list of db.table names to exclude
                            from schema/data export
  --per-table               Output individual files per object
  --region TEXT             Only export tables matching this region
  --format [sql|json|yaml]  Schema output format
  --include-permissions     Export CREATE ROLE, GRANT, and membership
                            statements
  --archive                 Compress output directory
  --diff TEXT               Compare output with existing SQL file
  --parallel                Enable parallel exports
  --data                    Export table data
  --data-format [sql|csv]   Data export format
  --data-split              Split each table into a separate file
  --data-limit INTEGER      Limit rows per table
  --data-compress           Compress CSV output
  --data-order TEXT         Order data by column(s)
  --data-order-desc         Order data descending
  --data-parallel           Parallel data export
  --data-order-strict       Fail if ordered column(s) not found
  --chunk-size INTEGER      Rows per CSV chunk
  --as-of-system-time TEXT  Read data at a consistent snapshot. The bare flag
                            pins one cluster_logical_timestamp(); or pass a
                            value like '-30s', a timestamp, or a decimal.
  --verify                  Verify exported chunk checksums
  --verify-strict           Stop if any checksum fails
  --out-dir TEXT            Output directory for all exports
  --print-connection        Print resolved database connection URL and exit
  --retry-count INTEGER     Number of retry attempts
  --retry-delay INTEGER     Initial retry delay in milliseconds
  --use-s3                  Enable S3 upload/download for data chunks
  --s3-bucket TEXT          S3 bucket to upload to or read from
  --s3-prefix TEXT          S3 key prefix path
  --s3-endpoint TEXT        Custom S3 endpoint (e.g. for Cohesity, MinIO)
  --s3-access-key TEXT      S3 access key
  --s3-secret-key TEXT      S3 secret key
  --help                    Show this message and exit.

docs.title: crdb-dump load
docs.text:
Usage:

crdb-dump load [OPTIONS]

Options:

  --db TEXT              Target database name  [required]
  --schema PATH          Schema SQL file to load
  --data-dir PATH        Directory containing manifest and data files
  --region TEXT          Only import tables from this region (matches manifest
                         region)
  --resume-log TEXT      Path to JSON file tracking loaded chunks
  --resume-log-dir PATH  Directory to store per-table resume logs (overrides
                         --resume-log)
  --resume-strict        Abort on first failed chunk when using resume
  --dry-run              Show what would be imported without executing
  --include-tables TEXT  Comma-separated list of fully-qualified tables to
                         include (e.g., movr.users)
  --exclude-tables TEXT  Comma-separated list of fully-qualified tables to
                         exclude
  --print-connection     Print resolved database connection URL and exit
  --parallel-load        Use parallel loading of chunks
  --validate-csv         Validate row/column match before COPY
  --retry-count INTEGER  Number of retry attempts
  --retry-delay INTEGER  Initial retry delay in milliseconds
  --use-s3               Enable S3 upload/download for data chunks
  --s3-bucket TEXT       S3 bucket to upload to or read from
  --s3-prefix TEXT       S3 key prefix path
  --s3-endpoint TEXT     Custom S3 endpoint (e.g. for Cohesity, MinIO)
  --s3-access-key TEXT   S3 access key
  --s3-secret-key TEXT   S3 secret key
  --help                 Show this message and exit.

docs.title: crdb-dump version
docs.text:
Show detailed version info.

Usage:

crdb-dump version [OPTIONS]

Options:

  --json  Output version info as JSON
  --help  Show this message and exit.

docs.title: Manifest Format
docs.text:
Each exported table gets a database.schema.table.manifest.json describing its chunks. Example:

{
  "table": "mydb.public.users",
  "region": "N/A",
  "chunks": [
    { "file": "mydb.public.users_001.csv", "rows": 1000, "sha256": "<hex>" },
    { "file": "mydb.public.users_002.csv", "rows": 1000, "sha256": "<hex>" }
  ]
}
Field Description table Fully-qualified database.schema.table name region Table locality (or N/A on single-region clusters) chunks[].file Chunk filename (relative to the data directory) chunks[].rows Row count in the chunk chunks[].sha256 SHA-256 checksum of the chunk file

The loader reads every *.manifest.json in --data-dir, loads each chunk via COPY, and records progress under a resume-log key derived from the manifest's table value.

docs.title: Naming Model
docs.text: CockroachDB objects are three-part: database.schema.table. crdb-dump uses this fully-qualified form consistently across SQL, filenames, manifests, and resume logs.

docs.title: --tables / --exclude-tables input
docs.text:
Input Interpreted as users <db>.public.users cpkit.tasks <db>.cpkit.tasks mydb.cpkit.tasks mydb.cpkit.tasks

A two-part name means schema.table (database from --db), not the legacy database.table. An explicit database prefix must match --db.

docs.title: Identifier quoting
docs.text: All identifiers (database, schema, table, column) are double-quoted when used in SQL, so mixed-case and reserved-word names work correctly and safely.

docs.title: File and manifest naming
docs.text:
Data files and manifests use the database.schema.table stem:

mydb.public.users_001.csv
mydb.public.users.manifest.json
mydb.cpkit.tasks_001.csv
mydb.cpkit.tasks.manifest.json

Resume-log keys are derived from the same database.schema.table name.
